---
title: About
---

## About Me

I am an Environmental Scientist and Data Analyst focused on turning complex environmental datasets into clear, decision-ready insights. My work combines environmental domain knowledge, geospatial analysis, and statistical programming to support better outcomes for ecosystems and communities.

I bring practical experience across national environmental reporting, climate data systems, and biodiversity science. I design reproducible analytical workflows in R and Python so evidence can be transparent, reusable, and useful to policy and operational teams.

## The Story Behind Penguinops

Before the world collectively discovered the joys of Zoom fatigue, I was a Marine Biologist. My days were spent pondering the mysteries of the ocean, wrangling algae, and occasionally wondering if my thesis would ever see the light of day. Then, the COVID-19 pandemic arrived, and so my career turned upside down.

During my PhD, my now wife (then my best friend) observed my uncanny resemblance to a Gentoo Penguin. Whether it was my waddling gait after a long day in the lab or my inexplicable fondness for collecting pebbles, she declared the penguin my spirit animal.

Since then, the penguin has become my personal brand. At work, I am known for data science; outside work, I am Pinguino (Spanish for Penguin). Thus, Penguinops was born: a tribute to my aquatic past, my dry sense of humor, and my ongoing quest to bring a little penguin magic to the world of data.

## Work Experience

### Kaitatari Hoahoa - Design Analyst

Tautauranga Aotearoa - Stats NZ | February 2023 - Present

- Technical lead for national data science initiatives focused on health and wellbeing data.
- Maintainer and developer of two data portals: Nga Tutohu Aotearoa and the COVID-19 Data Portal.
- Stats NZ shinyapps.io administrator for 200+ R Shiny apps.
- Statistics NZ GitHub administrator for private and public repositories.
- Contributor to analysis and communication products relevant to public policy and wellbeing outcomes.

### Climate Database Technician

National Institute of Water and Atmospheric Research (NIWA) | February 2021 - April 2022

- Managed and enhanced climate data systems for research and commercial use.
- Built an R Shiny app to explore weather station catalogues, error logs, and upload status.
- Improved quality control workflows for New Zealand's manual observer weather station network.
- Supported data integrity and operational reliability for climate and environmental monitoring systems.

### Molecular Biologist (Part-time)

National Institute of Water and Atmospheric Research (NIWA) | February 2021 - April 2022

- Conducted genetic sequencing and analysis for biodiversity and ecology projects.
- Produced hundreds of genetic sequences for the marine algae biodiversity programme.
- Worked at the intersection of lab science and data analysis for environmental research programmes.

## Education

- Doctoral Degree, Biological Sciences - The University of Auckland (Dec 2015 - Nov 2019)
- Master's in Marine and Coastal Sciences - UABCS (Aug 2011 - Jun 2013)
- Bachelor's Degree, Biology - Instituto Tecnologico de Bocal del Rio (Jun 2007 - May 2011)

## Skills

### Primary Skills

Environmental data analysis, R, Python, SQL, geospatial analysis (GIS), Git ecosystem, data visualization

### Secondary Skills

Shiny apps, Quarto, Docker, Linux, machine learning, API development, Jupyter Notebooks, LaTeX, Markdown, CI/CD, static site generators
